violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety that is registered with the RIDOH Center for Food Protection. Previous Certified Food Safety Manager License(s) were observed to be expired. Application for RIDOH CFP Certified Food Safety Manger License was printed and left with the PIC at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026

Eggplant Parmesan which was stored in the prep unit since previous day (on a plate above the cold zone) was observed held at 54 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Eggplant Parmesan was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for the following time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods: - Pasta cooked same day prior to inspection was observed cooling in the walk-n cooler in covered plastic containers at 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Pasta container lids were cracked/vented to allow for more rapid cooling at the time of inspection. - Garlic oil (Olive oil with roasted garlic, parmesan cheese, and spices) which was prepared same day as inspection, was observed in a covered plastic container placed in a shallow ice bath to cool down. At the time of inspection, the oil and other ingredients (roasted garlic, parmesan cheese, and spices) were separated and set for rapid cooling. Per the PIC, ingredients to be separated for proper storage until use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026

At the time of inspection, hand soap and paper towels for the hand sink located directly over/next to the prep sink. Soap and paper towels are to be relocated to minimize possible contamination of the prep sink or foods in the prep sink during times of use. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2026

Shellfish tags were observed not marked with the last date of use. Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, Tomato Sauce was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Food items set for cooling are to be placed in containers no greater than four inches deep for rapid cooling. Sauces were transferred to shallow pans at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2024

At the time of inspection, the following sources of possible contamination were observed... - Employee was observed to be washing hands in the food prep sink. Employees are to wash hands in the hand sink only to prevent possible contamination of the prep sink or foods in the prep sink at times of use. - Soap and paper towels for the hand sink located directly next to the prep sink are located directly over the prep sink. Soap and paper towels are to be relocated to minimize possible contamination of the prep sink or foods in the prep sink during times of use. - The hand sink and/or prep sink (located directly next to each other) are lacking splash guards. Splash guards are to be installed to prevent possible contamination of the prep sink during times of washing hands. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2024
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Proper storage if in-use utensils was discussed at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2024

The following non-food contact surfaces of equipment were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris... - Refrigeration door gaskets - Ice Machine shoot/guard - Walk-In Cooler fan shrouds New Violation. Correct By: 08/26/2024

Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 05/10/2023
A bug zapper with without a guard was observed above a food storage area. Per PIC, guard will be added to bug zapper to ensure bugs will stay inside. New Violation. Correct By: 05/24/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of bio-film. Per PIC, ice machine will be emptied and cleaned tonight. New Violation. Correct By: 05/10/2023

The dumpster outside is located on dirt. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2023

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the kitchen area on a speed rack. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 05/10/2023
